strengths: 15+ years of progressive full-stack experience with current active use of TypeScript, Node.js, and AI agent infrastructure (MCP frameworks, agentic workflows) directly matching primary stack and AI requirements
Deep testing culture: TDD advocacy, created testing workshops, and authored open-source testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), signaling strong engineering discipline
Demonstrated cloud and infrastructure experience — AWS CDK at Optilyz, Docker and Kubernetes for GISCollective — covering key secondary technical requirements
concerns: No mention of Nest.js anywhere in the resume despite it being the listed primary backend framework, nor any explicit reference to Vitest or Playwright testing frameworks
Kotlin and Java are absent from listed skills and job history, falling short of the secondary stack requirement and multi-language proficiency expectation
React experience appears in only one role (Optilyz); frontend depth at a senior level for a React-heavy stack is not strongly evidenced
response.justification: The candidate brings substantial full-stack seniority, a genuinely strong testing and craftsmanship ethos, and current hands-on experience with AI agent infrastructure — all of which align well with the role's core expectations. The primary gap is on the framework layer: Nest.js is the listed backend framework and there is no evidence the candidate has worked with it, and Kotlin/Java (secondary stack) are entirely absent. These are learnable gaps for an engineer of this caliber, but they represent real ramp-up time on the stack that defines day-to-day delivery.
